Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Bulletproof KenPom stats resolution for all CBB teams

The core bug was that get_kenpom_team_full\(\) used raw ESPN team names
to look up supplementary KenPom caches \(four_factors, height, misc\),
while only the ratings cache had fuzzy matching. This caused teams like
Mercyhurst, Le Moyne, CS Fullerton etc. to silently get empty stats.

Fix: Unified resolve_kenpom_key\(\) resolves any team name to the exact
KenPom cache key ONCE, then uses it for ALL cache lookups. Added ~75
new team entries to CBB_TEAM_NAME_MAP for ESPN shortDisplayName variants
and new D1 transitional teams.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(1 <<'PYEOF'
import os
os.environ.setdefault\('DATABASE_URL', os.environ.get\('DATABASE_URL', 'sqlite:///test.db'\)\)
from sports_app import app, resolve_kenpom_key, CBB_TEAM_NAME_MAP, normalize_cbb_team_name, normalize_cbb_team_for_kenpom

failing_teams = [
    'AR-Pine Bluff', 'Alabama St', 'UAlbany', 'Binghamton',
    'Tarleton St', 'Abilene Chrstn', 'MD Eastern', 'Howard',
    'New Orleans', 'Hou Christian', 'Coppin St', 'NC Central',
    'SE Louisiana', "N'Western St", 'UT Martin', 'SIUE',
    'Lehigh', 'Lafayette', 'Tennessee Tech', 'So Indiana',
    'Miss Valley St', 'Alabama A&M', 'SF Austin', 'UT Rio Grande',
    'C Connecticut', 'FDU', 'Stetson', 'FGCU',
    'Mercyhurst', 'Saint Francis', 'NJIT', 'Maine',
    'St Thomas \(MN\)', 'Kansas City', 'CA Baptist', 'Utah Tech',
    'Morgan St', 'SC State', 'Le Moyne', 'Stonehill', 'Lindenwood'
]

with app.app_context\(\):
    for team in failing_teams:
        key = resolve_kenpom_key\(team\)
        status = 'OK' if key else 'MISS'
        print\(f'[{status}] {team:25s} -> {key}'\)
PYEOF)

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Apply KenPom stats to ALL CBB games, not just those with Bovada lines

Previously KenPom data was only populated during odds processing,
so games without Bovada lines \(small conference matchups like
Mercyhurst, Le Moyne, SIUE, etc.\) had empty KenPom stats even
though the resolution worked. Now KenPom stats are applied to
every CBB game during fetch_games, before odds processing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(1 <<'PYEOF'
import os
os.environ.setdefault\('DATABASE_URL', os.environ.get\('DATABASE_URL', 'sqlite:///test.db'\)\)
from sports_app import \(app, db, Game, resolve_kenpom_key_cached, 
                         calculate_torvik_projection, fetch_all_kenpom_data\)
import pytz
from datetime import datetime

with app.app_context\(\):
    et = pytz.timezone\('America/New_York'\)
    today = datetime.now\(et\).date\(\)
    
    # Load KenPom data
    fetch_all_kenpom_data\(\)
    
    # Get today's CBB games
    cbb_games = Game.query.filter_by\(date=today, league='CBB'\).all\(\)
    
    # Test KenPom resolution for EVERY team in today's slate
    resolved = 0
    failed = []
    projection_ok = 0
    projection_fail = []
    
    for game in cbb_games:
        away_key = resolve_kenpom_key_cached\(game.away_team\)
        home_key = resolve_kenpom_key_cached\(game.home_team\)
        
        if away_key:
            resolved += 1
        else:
            failed.append\(f"AWAY: {game.away_team}"\)
        
        if home_key:
            resolved += 1
        else:
            failed.append\(f"HOME: {game.home_team}"\)
        
        # Test projection
        proj = calculate_torvik_projection\(game.away_team, game.home_team\)
        if proj:
            projection_ok += 1
        else:
            projection_fail.append\(f"{game.away_team} @ {game.home_team}"\)
    
    total_teams = len\(cbb_games\) * 2
    print\(f"\\n=== KenPom Resolution Report ==="\)
    print\(f"CBB games today: {len\(cbb_games\)}"\)
    print\(f"Teams resolved: {resolved}/{total_teams} \({resolved/total_teams*100:.1f}%\)"\)
    print\(f"Projections OK: {projection_ok}/{len\(cbb_games\)} \({projection_ok/len\(cbb_games\)*100:.1f}%\)"\)
    
    if failed:
        print\(f"\\nFailed resolutions \({len\(failed\)}\):"\)
        for f in failed:
            print\(f"  {f}"\)
    
    if projection_fail:
        print\(f"\\nFailed projections \({len\(projection_fail\)}\):"\)
        for f in projection_fail:
            print\(f"  {f}"\)
    
    print\(f"\\n=== Stats currently in DB ==="\)
    has_kenpom = sum\(1 for g in cbb_games if g.kenpom_away_efg is not None\)
    has_proj = sum\(1 for g in cbb_games if g.projected_total is not None\)
    has_torvik = sum\(1 for g in cbb_games if g.torvik_away_adj_o is not None\)
    print\(f"Games with KenPom 4factors: {has_kenpom}/{len\(cbb_games\)}"\)
    print\(f"Games with projections: {has_proj}/{len\(cbb_games\)}"\)
    print\(f"Games with efficiency ratings: {has_torvik}/{len\(cbb_games\)}"\)
PYEOF)

import os
os.environ.setdefault\('DATABASE_URL', os.environ.get\('DATABASE_URL', 'sqlite:///test.db'\)\)
from sports_app import app, torvik_cache, fetch_kenpom_ratings

with app.app_context\(\):
    fetch_kenpom_ratings\(\)
    # Search for northridge in cache
    for key in torvik_cache:
        if 'north' in key and \('ridge' in key or 'cal' in key or 'csun' in key or 'fullerton' in key or 'bakersfield' in key\):
            print\(f"  cache key: '{key}'"\)
    # Also search for cal st
    for key in torvik_cache:
        if key.startswith\('cal st'\):
            print\(f"  cache key: '{key}'"\)
PYEOF)

Bash(1 <<'PYEOF'
import os
os.environ.setdefault\('DATABASE_URL', os.environ.get\('DATABASE_URL', 'sqlite:///test.db'\)\)
import sports_app as sa

with sa.app.app_context\(\):
    sa.fetch_all_kenpom_data\(\)
    
    tests = ["Hawai\\u2019i", "CSU Northridge", "San José St", "CS Northridge", "CSUN"]
    for t in tests:
        key = sa.resolve_kenpom_key\(t\)
        proj_data = sa.get_kenpom_team_full\(t\)
        has_full = 'FULL' if proj_data and proj_data.get\('o_efg', 0\) > 0 else 'BASIC' if proj_data else 'NONE'
        print\(f"[{'OK' if key else 'MISS'}] {t!r:25s} -> key={key}, data={has_full}"\)
    
    # Full resolution test
    games = sa.Game.query.filter_by\(date=sa.datetime.now\(sa.pytz.timezone\('America/New_York'\)\).date\(\), league='CBB'\).all\(\)
    resolved = sum\(1 for g in games for t in [g.away_team, g.home_team] if sa.resolve_kenpom_key_cached\(t\)\)
    total = len\(games\) * 2
    
    proj_ok = sum\(1 for g in games if sa.calculate_torvik_projection\(g.away_team, g.home_team\)\)
    
    print\(f"\\n=== FINAL RESOLUTION: {resolved}/{total} teams \({resolved/total*100:.1f}%\), {proj_ok}/{len\(games\)} projections ==="\)
PYEOF)

Bash(1 <<'PYEOF'
import os
os.environ.setdefault\('DATABASE_URL', os.environ.get\('DATABASE_URL', 'sqlite:///test.db'\)\)
from vsin_scraper import get_all_vsin_data

result = get_all_vsin_data\('CBB'\)
print\(f"VSiN CBB success: {result['success']}"\)
print\(f"Total games: {result['count']}"\)
print\(f"Lines: {result.get\('lines_count', 0\)}, Splits: {result.get\('splits_count', 0\)}"\)

if result['success'] and result['data']:
    print\(f"\\nAll game keys:"\)
    for key in sorted\(result['data'].keys\(\)\):
        data = result['data'][key]
        away = data.get\('away_team', 'N/A'\)
        home = data.get\('home_team', 'N/A'\)
        tickets = f"T:{data.get\('tickets_away','?'\)}/{data.get\('tickets_home','?'\)}"
        money = f"M:{data.get\('money_away','?'\)}/{data.get\('money_home','?'\)}"
        print\(f"  {away:25s} @ {home:25s} | {tickets} {money}"\)
else:
    print\(f"Error: {result.get\('message', 'unknown'\)}"\)
PYEOF)

Bash(1 <<'PYEOF'
import os
os.environ.setdefault\('DATABASE_URL', os.environ.get\('DATABASE_URL', 'sqlite:///test.db'\)\)
import sports_app as sa

with sa.app.app_context\(\):
    # Test the match_vsin_data function directly
    from team_identity import normalize_team_name as identity_normalize
    
    # Simulate what the spreads page does
    vsin_nba_data = sa.MatchupIntelligence.fetch_rlm_data\('NBA'\)
    vsin_cbb_data = sa.MatchupIntelligence.fetch_rlm_data\('CBB'\)
    
    print\(f"VSiN NBA games: {len\(vsin_nba_data\)}"\)
    print\(f"VSiN CBB games: {len\(vsin_cbb_data\)}"\)
    
    # Check what the CBB data keys look like
    print\("\\nSample CBB keys:"\)
    for key in sorted\(vsin_cbb_data.keys\(\)\)[:10]:
        data = vsin_cbb_data[key]
        away = data.get\('away', {}\).get\('team', 'N/A'\)
        home = data.get\('home', {}\).get\('team', 'N/A'\)
        print\(f"  key='{key}' | {away} @ {home}"\)
    
    # Look for mercyhurst specifically
    print\("\\nSearching for Mercyhurst in VSiN data:"\)
    for key, data in vsin_cbb_data.items\(\):
        away = data.get\('away', {}\).get\('team', ''\) 
        home = data.get\('home', {}\).get\('team', ''\)
        if 'mercy' in key.lower\(\) or 'mercy' in away.lower\(\) or 'mercy' in home.lower\(\):
            print\(f"  FOUND: key='{key}' | {away} @ {home}"\)
            print\(f"    Full data: {data}"\)
PYEOF)
